exemplar.title: How does changing the length of suspension of a symmetrical bifilar pendulum affect the period?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does changing the length of suspension of a symmetrical bifilar pendulum affect the period?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are labelled with units and error bars, demonstrating clarity and precision
Instrumental uncertainties are stated and propagated to TÂ²
Linear regression with reported RÂ² shows engagement with data trends
weaknesses: Conceptual error in calculating g leads to a physically implausible result (~5.8 m/sÂ²)
Uncertainty in string length and other parameters is not propagated into the gradient or final g
Use of halfârange rather than standard deviation for five trials omits statistical rigour
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data is clear and precise (band 5), but consideration of uncertainties is incomplete (band 4) and dataâprocessing contains a significant inaccuracy (band 3). Overall performance aligns with the middle of the 3â4 band, warranting 4/6.

strengths: Research question is clearly stated within a specific context of a symmetrical bifilar pendulum
Detailed list of assumptions and apparatus setup supports reproducibility
Procedure includes numbered steps and photos, specifying string lengths and repeats
weaknesses: Minor omissions in operationalizing the release angle and motionâsensor alignment
Abrupt jump in derivation of period formula leaves intermediate steps unclear
Rationale for choosing the precise stringâseparation value is not fully explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands comfortably reach the 5â6 band (research question context and methodological considerations) and the dataâcollection description sits at the top of the 3â4 band. The work is clearly reproducible with only small ambiguities, matching the descriptor for a highâquality investigation. A few minor detail gaps prevent the top mark, so 5/6 is appropriate.

strengths: Conclusion restates the hypothesis and cites the high correlation coefficient to justify the TÂ²âL relationship
Discussion links nonâzero intercept to systematic error and compares experimental g to the accepted 9.81 m/sÂ²
Percentage difference is calculated and used to contextualise the result
weaknesses: Comparison between percent error and quoted uncertainty is not fully explored
Interpretation of the interceptâs sign could be clarified in terms of its physical effect
Broader scientific context (e.g., applications of bifilar pendulums) is only superficially addressed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is wellâjustified and consistent with the analysis (meeting the 5â6 band for relevance and support) and includes a relevant comparison to accepted values (justifying a high mark). Minor gaps in uncertainty discussion and broader context prevent the perfect score, so 5/6 is justified.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified and their impacts on period and intercept are explained
Improvements are realistic and linked to reducing identified errors (e.g., using a protractor clamp)
Suggests a range of targeted enhancements, showing depth of reflection
weaknesses: Some suggestions (vacuum chamber) exceed typical school resources
Quantitative assessment of improvements (e.g., amount of angle correction) is not provided
Minor contradictions in specified release angle noted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands reach the descriptor for explained weaknesses and explained improvements in the 5â6 band. The evaluation thoughtfully connects flaws to their corrections, but a few over-ambitious suggestions and a lack of quantification keep it from the highest mark, supporting a score of 5/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is clearly stated and immediately placed in the very specific context of a *symmetrical bifilar pendulum* whose suspension length is varied.  The introductory theory, free-body diagram and explicit statement of assumptions (small-angle, inextensible strings, negligible air resistance, etc.) show that the student understands and communicates the exact physical situation being explored rather than a vague âpendulumâ system.  Because both the mechanical set-up and the underlying physics are explicitly tied to the question, the work aligns with the 5â6 band; however, a concise statement of *why* the investigation is of interest (real-world application) is missing, so a mark of 5 rather than 6 is most fitting.

The student states instrumental uncertainties, propagates them to TÂ² using half-range, plots error bars and determines the uncertainty in the gradient and in g.  However, some significant omissions persist: the half-range method is used even when five trials allow a standard deviation; the uncertainty in L is never propagated into the gradient; and the motion-sensor timing uncertainty is assumed rather than evidenced.  Hence the work fits the middle 3-4 band, earning 4.

Relevant methodological considerationsâcontrol of rod geometry, constant separation r, string material, amplitude limits, air currents, horizontal alignmentâare *explained* rather than merely listed.  The rationale for the range of L values and number of trials is linked to minimising random error and preserving the SHM approximation, demonstrating that the student thought about sufficiency of data.  Minor gaps (no justification of 0.05 s sensor resolution) prevent full marks, so 5/6 is appropriate.

Specific methodological weaknesses are *explained* with their likely impact: string stretch causing a negative intercept, release-angle inconsistencies altering period, misalignment leading to wobble, and air resistance lowering g.  The narrative clearly links each flaw to how it would shift results, satisfying the descriptor for the 5-6 band; minor breadth gaps merit 5 rather than 6.

The student provides a numbered procedure; apparatus photographs; specification of 10 string lengths and 5 repeats; and details on how the period is extracted from motion-sensor traces.  Those instructions would allow another student to reproduce the investigation with only minor ambiguity (e.g., the precise method for ensuring the <15Â° release angle is not operationally defined, and alignment of the motion sensor is left to judgement).  Thus the description is largely reproducible but retains a few small omissions, corresponding to the upper end of the 3-4 band.

Processing steps (averaging, squaring periods, linear regression, calculation of g) are shown, but there is a conceptual error: the experimental gradient is used directly without propagating L uncertainty, leading to a value of g that is physically implausible (â5.8 m sâ»Â²).  This indicates an *inaccuracy* rather than mere omission.  Calculations are internally consistent but yield an erroneous physical constant, placing the work at the lower end of the 3-4 band (score 3).

Improvements (measuring length under tension with a caliper, protractor clamp for precise angle, limiting L range, use of vacuum chamber) are realistic within or just beyond a school context and are *explained* in terms of how they would reduce the identified errors.  The links from weakness â improvement â expected benefit are explicit, placing the work in the 5-6 band; slight over-ambition of the vacuum suggestion prevents a perfect 6, so 5 is awarded.

Data tables include headings, units and absolute uncertainties; averages and calculated TÂ² values are provided; the graph has labelled axes with units and error bars.  Although the formatting is occasionally cluttered, the recording is both *clear* (easy to follow) and *precise* (values given to consistent significant figures).  A minor issue is the inconsistent decimal places in some averages, therefore 5/6 rather than 6/6.

The student compares the experimental g to the accepted 9.81 m sâ»Â², calculates percentage difference, and links the large discrepancy to systematic errors such as string length measurement.  This constitutes a relevant comparison, but the discussion stops short of *fully* justifying the discrepancy through quantitative uncertainty analysis (the 40 % error is much larger than the quoted 12 % uncertainty, yet this inconsistency is not explored in depth).  Therefore it sits comfortably in the 3-4 band with 4 marks.

The conclusion restates the observed linear TÂ²âL relationship and explicitly cites the correlation coefficient from the graph.  It also comments on the non-zero intercept and links this to systematic error, demonstrating that the conclusion is *fully consistent* with the presented analysis.  Because the logical thread is strong but the numerical value of g is incorrect, a perfect 6 would be excessive; 5/6 reflects a justified conclusion drawn from the data provided.

annotations.comment: Introducing the final period formula abruptly leaves the derivation chain incomplete; consider summarizing key intermediate steps or referencing the full derivation to aid reproducibility.
Releasing the rod from a âconsistent angular displacementâ is vague. Specify the tool or gauge used to set the initial angle quantitatively and record its precision.
Step 1 of the method fixes string separation at 48.5 cm without stating how that precise value optimizes the experiment; explaining this choice would improve reproducibility.
Using maxâmin/2 to estimate $$\Delta T^2$$ is a valid half-range method, but with five trials a standard deviation approach would provide a statistically robust uncertainty estimate.
Stating the sensorâs $$\pm0.05\,$$s uncertainty as an assumption requires supporting calibration or datasheet evidence. Include a citation or calibration test to justify this value.
The plot of $$T^2$$ vs. $$L$$ includes clear axes labels, consistent error bars, and a reported RÂ² value, demonstrating precise and clear data communication.
The discussion of the non-zero intercept attributes it to systematic error but conflates negative sign with overestimation of period; clarify how a negative intercept physically shifts the line.
The 40.87% difference between experimental and accepted $$g$$ is reported, but discussion omits whether this lies within experimental uncertainty; compare percent difference with percent uncertainty.
The student clearly states the research question focusing on how suspension length affects the period of a symmetrical bifilar pendulum, anchoring the investigation in a well-defined physical setup.
The background section outlines the pendulum system, but could be strengthened by linking the investigation to real-world applications or phenomena to justify its broader significance.
The kinematics expression $$F = m\bigl(\frac{d^2s}{\mu^2}\bigr)$$ contains a notation error; the denominator should be $$dt^2$$. Correct differential notation is essential for clarity.
Assumptions are clearly listed (inextensible strings, small amplitude, negligible damping), which demonstrates awareness of conditions for simple harmonic motion. Well articulated.
The moment of inertia expression includes an unexplained factor â(4)^2.â Please clarify the origin of this factor or correct it if it is a transcription error.
The hypothesis states $$T\propto\sqrt L$$ but the accompanying equation $$T = \frac{2\pi}{L}$$ is incorrect. It should read $$T = \frac{2\pi}{r}\sqrt{\frac{IL}{mg}}$$ to align with theory.
Justification of the selected length range (55âcmâ100âcm) is persuasive; the explanation ties the range to manageable small-angle conditions and reduced random error.
Control of rod geometry is described, but quantifying how a small variation in moment of inertia would affect the period would strengthen the methodological rationale.

Preliminary measurement of pivot inertia and radii is precise, but uncertainty propagation to this I value is omitted; include propagation of $$\pm0.001\,$$m errors to justify $$\pm0.00006\,$$kgâmÂ².

The qualitative observation that longer strings introduce vertical oscillations is insightful. Consider quantifying this effect (e.g. amplitude of vertical motion) to assess its impact on period precision.
Table 1 lists averages to inconsistent decimal places. Standardize all period averages to the same significant figures to maintain precision and clarity.
Processed TÂ² data table omits propagation of string-length uncertainty into the gradient calculation. Including $$\Delta L$$ in the error bars will improve the rigour of uncertainty analysis.
The calculated experimental $$g_{exp}=5.82049$$âm/sÂ² is physically implausible; this discrepancy indicates a conceptual or calculation error in gradient application or unit conversion.
Uncertainty propagation for $$g$$ omits contributions from $$\Delta I$$, $$\Delta m$$, and $$\Delta r$$. Propagate all sources to give a comprehensive $$\pm0.7$$âm/sÂ² estimate.
The conclusion effectively restates the hypothesis and uses the high correlation coefficient (0.9942) to support the linear relationship observed.
Highlighting the motion sensor to reduce human timing error is well noted; this demonstrates thoughtful procedural strength in minimizing random error.
The proposed use of a caliper to measure string length under tension is realistic. To improve, specify the protocol for measuring under load to directly account for string stretch.
Specifying the initial angle as â90Â°â instead of â<15Â°â contradicts the small-angle approximation; correct this to reflect the intended release angle and avoid confusion.
Center-of-rotation misalignment was noted, but quantifying wobble amplitude (e.g. degrees off horizontal) would allow assessment of its impact on torque and period.
Identifying a random-error outlier at 0.70âm is good practice. For improvement, apply an outlier test (e.g. Grubbsâ test) and increase trial number to confirm statistical consistency.
Suggesting a vacuum chamber for eliminating air resistance shows ambition; for more feasible improvements, consider a streamlined rod cover or use denser material to reduce drag in a school setting.
The proposed extension comparing pendulum materials is well-conceived and ties experimental outcomes to material properties, offering a strong bridge to engineering applications.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
